name: SOL Deep Value Reclaim v1
thesis: SOL 4h is the best-fit primary lane for testing trend pullback continuation after a deeper value reclaim into structural support. The edge should come from a meaningful reclaim after overshoot, not from shallow trend-chasing.
invalidation_condition: Abort if reclaim setups remain too rare, if the support-reclaim and continuation filters conflict, or if continuation fails to pay after costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price overshoots below value, reclaims structural support, and then continues for a limited second leg once the reclaim is accepted.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In mixed chop/transition conditions, deeper overshoots can create reflexive reclaims that continue farther than a plain mean-reversion snapback once support is regained.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: SOL 4h is the live primary lane and a suitable place to test a deeper reclaim because the asset tends to overshoot local balance enough to make reclaim structure visible.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If SOL remains purely rotational, reclaimed support may only produce a one-bar bounce rather than continuation.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries exist but continuation is too short, cut runner ambition before tightening the reclaim trigger.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; overshoot, reclaim, and continuation confirmation are sequential
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by using a broad deep-reclaim definition rather than rare capitulation-only thresholds
